description: Rubric used by the Judge LLM to evaluate the quality of Film Lab's per-photo analysis outputs. Edit this file to change how quality is measured — the Judge reads it at runtime.

criteria.name: Title Specificity
criteria.description: Is the title specific and evocative to this exact frame, or generic and interchangeable?

levels.1: Generic — could apply to any photograph (e.g. 'Golden Light', 'Morning Mist')
levels.2: Slightly specific but still interchangeable with similar photos
levels.3: References a recognizable element of the photo but lacks originality
levels.4: Evocative and clearly tied to something unique in this frame
levels.5: Poetic and unexpected — could only belong to this particular image

criteria.name: Feedback Precision
criteria.description: Is the technical feedback precise, causal, and actionable — or vague and obvious?

levels.1: Vague or obvious — adds no value beyond looking at the photo
levels.2: Names a technical issue but without identifying cause or offering direction
levels.4: Precise — identifies cause, effect, and implies a corrective direction
levels.5: Surgical — names exact technical cause, its effect on the image, and a clear improvement path

criteria.name: Internal Consistency
criteria.description: Do the technical notes (exposure, lighting, composition) align with and support the overall teacherFeedback?

levels.1: Technical notes directly contradict the feedback
levels.2: Partially aligned — at least one note contradicts or undermines the feedback
levels.3: Mostly consistent with minor gaps or redundancy
levels.4: Well-aligned — each note contributes to a coherent assessment
levels.5: Perfectly consistent — title, feedback, and technical notes form a single unified critique

improvement_rule: Any criterion with an average score below passing_threshold should be listed as an area for improvement in the evaluation report. Photos scoring below flag_threshold on any criterion should be individually flagged.
